root_cause.text: The Jenkins CLI subsystem lacks validation or filtering of classes during object deserialization, allowing attackers to instantiate arbitrary malicious classes via crafted serialized payloads.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces the standard `ObjectInputStream(in)` with `ObjectInputStreamEx(in, getClass().getClassLoader(), ClassFilter.DEFAULT)` in `hudson.cli.Connection.readObject()`, introducing a class filter to prevent the deserialization of prohibited classes.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The Jenkins CLI subsystem lacks validation or filtering of classes during object deserialization, allowing attackers to instantiate arbitrary malicious classes via crafted serialized payloads.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces the standard `ObjectInputStream(in)` with `ObjectInputStreamEx(in, getClass().getClassLoader(), ClassFilter.DEFAULT)` in `hudson.cli.Connection.readObject()`, introducing a class filter to prevent the deserialization of prohibited classes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Import classes necessary for secure object deserialization filtering.
intent_record.what: Added import statements for hudson.remoting.ClassFilter and hudson.remoting.ObjectInputStreamEx.
intent_record.how: Makes secure deserialization classes available for use within this file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Secure object deserialization by introducing a class filter.
intent_record.what: Replaced ObjectInputStream with ObjectInputStreamEx and added a ClassFilter.
intent_record.how: Restricts deserialization to prevent malicious class instantiation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Import the Security218 class for use in testing.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for org.codehaus.groovy.runtime.Security218.
intent_record.how: Makes the Security218 class available for use within this test class.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add testSecurity218 to verify that deserializing restricted classes throws a SecurityException.
intent_record.what: Added a new test method testSecurity218 that writes a Security218 object and expects a SecurityException when reading it.
intent_record.how: Executes object serialization and expects a SecurityException during deserialization, asserting that the exception message contains the class name.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a test payload class to verify security restrictions on prohibited package names.
intent_record.what: A new serializable test class named Security218 was added to the org.codehaus.groovy.runtime package.
intent_record.how: It provides a test payload to verify security restrictions on prohibited package names during execution.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add necessary imports for FieldSetter and AnnotatedElement to support upcoming code changes.
intent_record.what: Added import statements for FieldSetter and AnnotatedElement.
intent_record.how: Resolves compilation dependencies by making FieldSetter and AnnotatedElement available for use in the class.

intent_record.intent_summary: Implement missing interface methods in the anonymous class to satisfy contract updates.
intent_record.what: Added asFieldSetter() returning null and asAnnotatedElement() returning the annotated element 'p' to an anonymous class.
intent_record.how: Fulfills new interface contract requirements, ensuring the anonymous class compiles and behaves correctly when these methods are called.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add missing hidden() method delegation to annotation wrapper.
intent_record.what: Added an overridden hidden() method to the class.
intent_record.how: Delegates the hidden() method call to the underlying base object.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add import for AnnotatedElement to support reflection operations.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for java.lang.reflect.AnnotatedElement.
intent_record.how: Makes the AnnotatedElement interface available for use within the class, likely to support reflection-based annotation processing.

intent_record.intent_summary: Make the Parameter class implement AnnotatedElement to support annotation processing.
intent_record.what: The Parameter class was modified to implement the AnnotatedElement interface.
intent_record.how: This allows Parameter objects to be utilized in contexts that require an AnnotatedElement, enabling standardized annotation retrieval.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add missing annotation retrieval methods to the custom parameter representation class.
intent_record.what: Added four overridden methods related to annotation retrieval (isAnnotationPresent, getAnnotation, getAnnotations, getDeclaredAnnotations).
intent_record.how: Provides custom implementations for checking and retrieving annotations by delegating to internal annotation() and annotations() helper methods.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update the remoting dependency to a newer snapshot version.
intent_record.what: Updated the version of the remoting dependency from 2.47 to 2.53-20151108.042522-3.
intent_record.how: The project will now be built and run using the newer version of the remoting library, incorporating any changes or fixes from that release.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update import statements to include required classes for testing and security checks.
intent_record.what: Added new import statements for Functions, Security218, and Assert, and adjusted formatting.
intent_record.how: Provides the necessary class dependencies for the file to utilize these specific components during execution.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add static import for JUnit's fail method to simplify test code.
intent_record.what: Added a static import for org.junit.Assert.fail.
intent_record.how: Allows the fail() method to be used directly in the test class without fully qualifying the class name.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add tests to verify the SECURITY-218 vulnerability patch.
intent_record.what: Added two new test methods for security218.
intent_record.how: Verifies that the system rejects the execution of the Security218 class over CLI connections.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add integration tests to verify that the SECURITY-218 vulnerability fix applies correctly to Jenkins slaves.
intent_record.what: Added a new test class Security218Test to verify security fixes for Jenkins slaves.
intent_record.how: Provides test methods for both dumb and JNLP slaves that attempt to send a malicious response back to the master, expecting a SecurityException to be thrown, thereby confirming the connection is appropriately rejected.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a test payload class to verify the blocking of prohibited Groovy package names.
intent_record.what: Added a new test class named Security218 in the org.codehaus.groovy.runtime package.
intent_record.how: Provides a mock Callable payload to test the rejection of classes from prohibited package names during Jenkins remoting.

grouping_result.intent: Update import statements to include required classes for testing and security checks.
Secure object deserialization by introducing a class filter.
Add integration tests to verify that the SECURITY-218 vulnerability fix applies correctly to Jenkins slaves.
Add necessary imports for FieldSetter and AnnotatedElement to support upcoming code changes.
Implement missing interface methods in the anonymous class to satisfy contract updates.
Add missing hidden() method delegation to annotation wrapper.
Add import for AnnotatedElement to support reflection operations.
Add missing annotation retrieval methods to the custom parameter representation class.
Update the remoting dependency to a newer snapshot version.
Add static import for JUnit's fail method to simplify test code.
Add a test payload class to verify the blocking of prohibited Groovy package names.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add tests and related imports to verify secure object deserialization filtering and restrictions on prohibited classes.
Add integration tests and necessary imports to verify the SECURITY-218 vulnerability patch.
Implement AnnotatedElement and add annotation retrieval methods to the Parameter class.

relevance_review.reason: The group adds tests to verify the exact fix for the vulnerability, which is the secure filtering and restriction of classes during object deserialization.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability by introducing a class filter for object deserialization, which mitigates the lack of validation described in the root cause.
The group adds integration tests specifically designed to verify the fix for the SECURITY-218 vulnerability, which is the direct result of addressing the root cause.
The group only adds import statements, which are setup or refactoring changes and do not directly address the deserialization vulnerability.
The group's changes focus on implementing missing interface methods to satisfy contract updates, which is unrelated to addressing the deserialization vulnerability in the CLI subsystem.
Adding a missing hidden() method delegation to an annotation wrapper is unrelated to addressing object deserialization vulnerabilities in the Jenkins CLI.
Adding an import statement for AnnotatedElement is a minor code refactoring/support change and does not directly address the deserialization vulnerability.

The group focuses on implementing AnnotatedElement and adding annotation retrieval methods to a Parameter class, which is completely unrelated to the root cause of lacking validation during object deserialization in the Jenkins CLI subsystem.

Updating the remoting dependency is directly related to addressing the deserialization vulnerability, as the updated library likely contains the underlying fix or necessary class filtering mechanisms.
The group only adds a static import for a test method, which is a minor code cleanup and unrelated to addressing the deserialization vulnerability.
The group adds a test payload to verify the blocking of prohibited classes, directly testing the fix for the deserialization vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: Creates an ObjectInputStream without any filtering mechanisms, which directly enables the deserialization vulnerability.
Reads the object from the unfiltered stream, leading to arbitrary class instantiation.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement creates an ObjectInputStream without any class filtering mechanisms, directly allowing the deserialization of arbitrary malicious classes.
This statement triggers the deserialization process on the unfiltered stream, leading to arbitrary class instantiation.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Implemented SecurityRealm-independent authentication mechanism,
and an implementation around SSH keys.

changelog will b
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 0d4117e48fe1152c07ab49dd9b662eb14a459e58
Author: Kohsuke Kawaguchi <kk@kohsuke.org>
Date:   Thu Jun 23 12:13:01 2011 -0700

    Implemented SecurityRealm-independent authentication mechanism,
    and an implementation around SSH keys.
    
    changelog will be updated when the server-side authentication module
    is added.

diff --git a/cli/src/main/java/hudson/cli/Connection.java b/cli/src/main/java/hudson/cli/Connection.java
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..543e6acb3e
--- /dev/n
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `readObject()` uses a standard `ObjectInputStream` to deserialize objects from the input stream (`return (T)ois.readObject();`) without any class validation, filtering, or look-ahead mechanisms. This directly exposes the application to arbitrary object deserialization vulnerabilities.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Implemented SecurityRealm-independent authentication mechanism,
and an implementation around SSH keys.

changelog will b
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 0d4117e48fe1152c07ab49dd9b662eb14a459e58
Author: Kohsuke Kawaguchi <kk@kohsuke.org>
Date:   Thu Jun 23 12:13:01 2011 -0700

    Implemented SecurityRealm-independent authentication mechanism,
    and an implementation around SSH keys.
    
    changelog will be updated when the server-side authentication module
    is added.

diff --git a/cli/src/main/java/hudson/cli/Connection.java b/cli/src/main/java/hudson/cli/Connection.java
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..543e6acb3e
--- /dev/n
bic_trace_step.reason: The `readObject()` method uses a plain `ObjectInputStream` to deserialize objects directly from the input stream (`ObjectInputStream ois = new ObjectInputStream(in); return (T)ois.readObject();`). It lacks any class validation or filtering, introducing the deserialization vulnerability described in the root cause.
